en.extract:
Brass's friarbird (Philemon brassi) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to West Papua, Indonesia.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and swamps. It is threatened by habitat loss.
The common name and Latin binomial commemorate the Australian botanist, Leonard J. Brass (1900-1971), who worked for the Queensland Herbarium .

'''Brass's friarbird''' ('''''Philemon brassi''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[West Papua (region)|West Papua]], [[Indonesia]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and [[swamp]]s. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

The common name and Latin binomial commemorate the Australian botanist, [[Leonard J. Brass]] (1900-1971), who worked for the [[Queensland Herbarium]] .<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=Whose Bird? Men and Women Commemorated in the Common Names of Birds|year=2003|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|page=63|author2=Watkins, Michael}}</ref>
